title: sorbus chamaemespilus subsp. sudetica
intro: Sorbus chamaemespilus subsp. sudetica, also known as the mountain ash or rowan, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The mountain ash is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and digestive issues.
